If \(a\) and \(b\) are constants such that \((x+8)(x+a)+b \equiv x^2+5a(x+3)\), then \(b=\)
A. \(-14\).
B. \(-2\).
C. \(2\).
D. \(14\).

Let \(k\) be a constant. Solve the equation \(x^2+4x=k^2-2k-3\).
A. \(x=k-3\) or \(x=-k-1\)
B. \(x=k-3\) or \(x=-k+1\)
C. \(x=k+3\) or \(x=-k-1\)
D. \(x=k+3\) or \(x=-k+1\)

If \(x=5.67\) (correct to \(2\) decimal places), find the range of values of \(x\).
A. \(5.66 \lt x \le 5.68\)
B. \(5.66 \le x \lt 5.68\)
C. \(5.665 \lt x \le 5.675\)
D. \(5.665 \le x \lt 5.675\)

The solution of \(4y+1 \lt 5y-3 \le 8y-9\) is
A. \(y \gt -4\).
B. \(y \ge -2\).
C. \(y \ge 2\).
D. \(y \gt 4\).

Let \(p(x)=nx^3-3nx+36\), where \(n\) is a constant. If \(x+3\) is a factor of \(p(x)\), then \(p(3)=\)
A. \(-2\).
B. \(0\).
C. \(2\).
D. \(72\).

A sum of \(\$40\,000\) is deposited at an interest rate of \(3\%\) per annum for \(5\) years compounded half-yearly. Find the amount correct to the nearest dollar.
A. \(\$46\,000\)
B. \(\$46\,371\)
C. \(\$46\,422\)
D. \(\$46\,465\)

If \(\alpha,\beta\) and \(\gamma\) are non-zero constants such that \((\alpha+2\beta):(\beta+2\gamma):(\gamma+2\alpha)=4:9:5\), then \(\alpha:\beta=\)
A. \(2:5\).
B. \(5:2\).
C. \(128:149\).
D. \(149:128\).

It is given that \(z\) varies directly as the cube of \(x\) and inversely as the square of \(y\). When \(x=3\) and \(y=6\), \(z=3\). When \(x=5\) and \(y=2\), \(z=\)
A. \(5\).
B. \(25\).
C. \(125\).
D. \(243\).

Let \(a_n\) be the \(n\)th term of a sequence. If \(a_2=3\), \(a_5=41\) and \(a_{n+2}=2a_{n+1}+a_n\) for any positive integer \(n\), then \(a_6=\)
A. \(99\).
B. \(101\).
C. \(239\).
D. \(243\).

In the figure, the equation of the straight line \(L\) is \(px+qy=7\). Which of the following is/are true?

I. \(p \gt 7\)
II. \(q \gt 7\)
III. \(q \gt p\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. I and III only
D. II and III only

The perimeter of the sector \(OMN\) is \(12\pi\text{ cm}\), where \(O\) is the centre of the sector \(OMN\). If the radius of the sector \(OMN\) is \(3\pi\text{ cm}\), which of the following are true?
I. The area of the sector \(OMN\) is \(9\pi^2\text{ cm}^2\).
II. The perimeter of \(\Delta OMN\) is less than \(35\text{ cm}\).
III. The angle of the sector \(OMN\) is greater than \(100^\circ\).
A. I and II only
B. I and III only
C. II and III only
D. I, II and III

The height and the total surface area of a solid right circular cylinder are \(35\text{ cm}\) and \(492\pi\text{ cm}^2\) respectively. If the radius of a solid sphere is equal to the base radius of the circular cylinder, then the volume of the sphere is
A. \(144\pi\text{ cm}^3\).
B. \(288\pi\text{ cm}^3\).
C. \(576\pi\text{ cm}^3\).
D. \(864\pi\text{ cm}^3\).

In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a parallelogram. Let \(E\), \(F\) and \(G\) be points lying on \(AB\), \(AD\) and \(CD\) respectively such that \(BE=3AE\), \(2DF=3AF\) and \(DG=2CG\). It is given that \(BC\) produced and \(EG\) produced meet at the point \(H\). If the area of \(\Delta CGH\) is \(16\text{ cm}^2\), then the area of the quadrilateral \(DFEG\) is

A. \(46\text{ cm}^2\).
B. \(49\text{ cm}^2\).
C. \(105\text{ cm}^2\).
D. \(115\text{ cm}^2\).

The figure shows the triangle \(WXY\). Let \(Z\) be a point lying on \(WY\) such that \(WY:XY=XY:YZ\). If \(WX=65\text{ cm}\), \(WZ=25\text{ cm}\) and \(XZ=60\text{ cm}\), then \(XY=\)

A. \(131\text{ cm}\).
B. \(144\text{ cm}\).
C. \(156\text{ cm}\).
D. \(169\text{ cm}\).

In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a square. Let \(E\) be a point such that \(CDEF\) is a rhombus, where \(F\) is a point lying on \(AC\) produced. \(DF\) is produced to the point \(G\) such that \(AF \mathbin{\!/\mkern-5mu/\!} BG\). Which of the following are true?

I. \(DF=FG\)
II. \(\Delta BFG \sim \Delta DEF\)
III. \(\angle ABG+\angle BFD=180^\circ\)
A. I and II only
B. I and III only
C. II and III only
D. I, II and III

It is given that \(PQRS\) is a trapezium with \(PQ \mathbin{\!/\mkern-5mu/\!} SR\). If \(PS=41\text{ cm}\), \(RS=53\text{ cm}\), \(\angle PSR=120^\circ\) and \(\angle QRS=150^\circ\), then \(PQ=\)
A. \(82\text{ cm}\).
B. \(100\text{ cm}\).
C. \(106\text{ cm}\).
D. \(135\text{ cm}\).

In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a rectangle. It is given that \(AE=20\text{ cm}\) and \(\angle AED=90^\circ\). If the area of \(\Delta ADE\) is \(150\text{ cm}^2\), then the perpendicular distance from \(E\) to \(CD\) is

A. \(8\text{ cm}\).
B. \(9\text{ cm}\).
C. \(12\text{ cm}\).
D. \(15\text{ cm}\).

In the figure, \(RSTUV\) is a circle. It is given that \(RT \mathbin{\!/\mkern-5mu/\!} VU\) and \(RT\) is the angle bisector of \(\angle SRU\). If \(\angle RTS=\angle URV=33^\circ\), then \(\angle RUT=\)

A. \(36^\circ\).
B. \(49^\circ\).
C. \(65^\circ\).
D. \(71^\circ\).

The figure shows the quadrilateral \(ABCD\). If \(\angle ABC+\angle ADC=\angle ACD=\angle BAC=90^\circ\), which of the following must be true?

A. \(\tan\angle ACB=\dfrac{AB}{AD}\)
B. \(\tan\angle ACB=\dfrac{AB}{CD}\)
C. \(\tan\angle ADC=\dfrac{BC}{AD}\)
D. \(\tan\angle ADC=\dfrac{BC}{CD}\)

The polar coordinates of the points \(X\), \(Y\) and \(Z\) are \((1,20^\circ)\), \((2,80^\circ)\) and \((r,\theta)\) respectively, where \(20^\circ \lt \theta \lt 80^\circ\). If \(\Delta XYZ\) is an equilateral triangle, find \(r\).
A. \(\sqrt{3}\)
B. \(\sqrt{5}\)
C. \(\sqrt{7}\)
D. \(\sqrt{10}\)

Denote the origin by \(O\). The coordinates of the point \(A\) are \((a,2a)\), where \(a \gt 0\). Let \(P\) be a moving point in the rectangular coordinate plane such that \(AP=OA\). The locus of \(P\) is a
A. point.
B. circle.
C. rhombus.
D. straight line.

The straight lines \(L_1\) and \(L_2\) are perpendicular to each other. The equations of \(L_1\) and \(L_2\) are \(3x+4y-20=0\) and \(mx+ny-20=0\) respectively, where \(m\) and \(n\) are constants. Suppose that \(L_1\) and \(L_2\) cut the \(x\)-axis at the points \(A\) and \(B\) respectively. Let \(C\) be the point of intersection of \(L_1\) and \(L_2\). It is given that \(C\) lies above the \(x\)-axis. If the area of \(\Delta ABC\) is \(6\), find \(n\).
A. \(-12\)
B. \(-9\)
C. \(9\)
D. \(12\)

The coordinates of the centre of the circle \(C\) are \((7,-5)\). If \(C\) cuts the \(x\)-axis at the points \(P\) and \(Q\) such that \(PQ=24\), then the equation of \(C\) is
A. \(x^2+y^2-14x+10y-95=0\).
B. \(x^2+y^2+14x-10y-95=0\).
C. \(x^2+y^2-14x+10y-119=0\).
D. \(x^2+y^2+14x-10y-119=0\).

A box contains six cards numbered \(2,2,2,3,4\) and \(5\) respectively. In a game, a number is randomly drawn from the box and a certain number of tokens will be got according to the following table:
Number drawn \(2\) \(3\) \(4\) \(5\)
Number of tokens got \(10\) \(15\) \(25\) \(50\)
Find the expected number of tokens got in the game.
A. \(3\)
B. \(4\)
C. \(20\)
D. \(25\)

The bar chart below shows the distribution of the numbers of books read by some teachers in a certain month. Find the inter-quartile range of the distribution.

A. \(1\)
B. \(2\)
C. \(3\)
D. \(4\)

Consider the following data:
\(\alpha,\ \beta,\ -4,\ -3,\ 1,\ 1,\ 1,\ 4\)
Denote the mode and the median of the above data by \(s\) and \(t\) respectively. If the mean and the range of the above data are \(0\) and \(10\) respectively, which of the following are true?
I. \(s=1\)
II. \(t=-1\)
III. \(\alpha+\beta=0\)
A. I and II only
B. I and III only
C. II and III only
D. I, II and III

It is given that \(\log_5 y\) is a linear function of \(\log_{25} x\). The intercepts on the vertical axis and on the horizontal axis of the graph of the linear function are \(12\) and \(2\) respectively. If \(y=mx^n\), then \(n=\)
A. \(-12\).
B. \(-4\).
C. \(-3\).
D. \(-1\).

On the same rectangular coordinate system, the graph of \(y=\log_a x\) cuts the graph of \(y=a^x\) and the \(x\)-axis at the points \(P\) and \(Q\) respectively, where \(a\) is a positive constant. Denote the origin by \(O\). Which of the following are true?
I. \(a \lt 1\)
II. \(OQ \gt a\)
III. \(\angle POQ=45^\circ\)
A. I and II only
B. I and III only
C. II and III only
D. I, II and III

Consider the following system of inequalities:
\[\begin{cases}x \le 11\\4x+5y-19 \ge 0\\7x-6y+11 \le 0\end{cases}\]
Let \(D\) be the region which represents the solution of the above system of inequalities. If \((x,y)\) is a point lying in \(D\), then the greatest value of \(8x-6y+11\) is
A. \(1\).
B. \(11\).
C. \(15\).
D. \(129\).

Let \(p\), \(q\) and \(r\) be non-zero real numbers. If \(p\), \(q\), \(r\) is an arithmetic sequence, which of the following must be true?
I. \(3^p,3^q,3^r\) is a geometric sequence.
II. \(\dfrac{5}{p},\dfrac{5}{q},\dfrac{5}{r}\) is a geometric sequence.
III. \(p-q,\ q-r,\ r-p\) is an arithmetic sequence.
A. I only
B. II only
C. I and III only
D. II and III only

In the figure, \(AC\) is a diameter of the circle \(ABCD\). Denote the point of intersection of \(AC\) and \(BD\) by \(E\). It is given that \(TD\) is the tangent to the circle at \(D\). If \(\angle BEC=96^\circ\) and \(\angle CDT=41^\circ\), then \(\angle CDE=\)

A. \(47^\circ\).
B. \(48^\circ\).
C. \(52^\circ\).
D. \(55^\circ\).

For \(90^\circ \lt \theta \lt 270^\circ\), how many roots does the equation \(\tan^3\theta = 2\tan\theta\) have?
A. \(2\)
B. \(3\)
C. \(4\)
D. \(5\)

\(PQRS\) is a regular tetrahedron. Find the angle between \(PQ\) and \(\Delta QRS\) correct to the nearest degree.
A. \(35^\circ\)
B. \(55^\circ\)
C. \(60^\circ\)
D. \(71^\circ\)

Denote the origin by \(O\). The coordinates of the point \(U\) are \((20,0)\). Let \(V\) be a point lying on the positive \(y\)-axis such that the \(x\)-coordinate of the in-centre of \(\Delta OUV\) is \(6\). Find the area of \(\Delta OUV\).
A. \(70\)
B. \(87\)
C. \(210\)
D. \(250\)

A committee is formed by \(2\) managers, \(4\) officers and \(12\) clerks. If \(7\) members are selected from the committee to form a working group consisting of at least \(1\) manager, how many different working groups can be formed?
A. \(16\,016\)
B. \(20\,384\)
C. \(22\,880\)
D. \(31\,824\)

There are \(9\) cans of apple juice and \(4\) cans of grape juice in a bag. If \(6\) cans are randomly chosen from the bag at the same time, find the probability that at most \(3\) cans of grape juice are chosen.
A. \(\dfrac{9}{13}\)
B. \(\dfrac{133}{143}\)
C. \(\dfrac{140}{143}\)
D. \(\dfrac{714}{715}\)

The standard scores of a boy and a girl in a Mathematics test are \(-2\) and \(z\) respectively. The standard deviation of the scores of the Mathematics test is \(2\) marks. If the difference of the test score of the boy and the test score of the girl is \(6\) marks, find \(z\).
A. \(-5\) or \(1\)
B. \(-5\) or \(3\)
C. \(-3\) or \(1\)
D. \(-3\) or \(3\)

It is given that \(a\), \(b\), \(c\) and \(d\) are four distinct real numbers. Let \(m_1\), \(r_1\) and \(v_1\) be the mean, the range and the variance of the group of numbers \(\{a,b,c,d\}\) respectively, while \(m_2\), \(r_2\) and \(v_2\) be the mean, the range and the variance of the group of numbers \(\{2a,2b,2c,2d\}\) respectively. Denote the mean, the range and the variance of the group of numbers \(\{a+3,b+3,c+3,d+3\}\) by \(m_3\), \(r_3\) and \(v_3\) respectively. Which of the following are true?
I. \(m_1+m_3 \gt m_2\)
II. \(r_1+r_3=r_2\)
III. \(v_1+v_3 \lt v_2\)
A. I and II only
B. I and III only
C. II and III only
D. I, II and III
